title: Will President Trump send ICE agents to Boston’s Logan Airport?
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: President Trump plans to send ICE agents to airports amid TSA staffing shortages due to ongoing government shutdown.
ICE deployment aims to address security and alleviate airport delays during the shutdown, despite no confirmed New England presence.
Logan Airport reports no current staffing issues, with TSA union leader questioning the necessity of ICE intervention in New England.

title: Is Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman a legitimate Vezina candidate? The numbers speak for themselves.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman made 42 saves in Boston Bruins' crucial 4-2 victory over the Detroit Red Wings.
Swayman ranks second in NHL for goals saved above expected, with a score of 27.1 per MoneyPuck.
Bruins defense struggles, allowing high-danger chances; Swayman's performance key to playoff hopes.

title: Celtics legend Paul Pierce continues to make a case for Jaylen Brown as NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce believes Jaylen Brown deserves consideration for NBA MVP due to his outstanding season with the Celtics.
Brown averages 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game, all career-highs, while playing excellent defense.
The Celtics hold the second place in the Eastern Conference with a 47-23 record, thanks in large part to Brown's leadership.

title: Plymouth police officer on paid leave amid criminal investigation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Plymouth police officer Samantha Pelrine is on paid leave amid a State Police criminal investigation into her off-duty conduct.
The investigation led by Massachusetts State Police was communicated to Plymouth police last week, resulting in Pelrine's administrative leave.
The Plymouth Police Department is also conducting an internal investigation while emphasizing their commitment to accountability and transparency.

title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony predicted to win AL MVP by The Athletic's Eno Sarris for the 2026 MLB season.
The 21-year-old Red Sox outfielder impressed at the 2026 World Baseball Classic, boosting his MVP candidacy.
Anthony's rookie season in 2025 ended with a .292 average, eight home runs, and 32 RBI over 71 games.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run Friday morning near Southampton Street and Bradston Street in Roxbury.
The victim was transported to a local hospital where they were pronounced dead, according to Boston police.
The driver fled the scene, and while the vehicle was found, no arrests have been made yet.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Rain showers are expected to transition to wet snow in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and Connecticut Monday morning.
The National Weather Service predicts up to 2 inches of wet snow, especially near Interstate 90.
Snow showers Monday night could result in a coating to an inch on grassy surfaces, particularly in hilly areas.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood police officer Shawn Willman was arrested for operating under the influence while off duty.
Willman is on administrative leave pending the outcome of the criminal case and an internal investigation.
Norwood Police Department announced the charges and emphasized their high standards for officer conduct.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, from Marston Mills, was charged after allegedly stabbing a friend at a Miami Beach bachelor party.
Incident occurred when Dickerson and Brian Gordon, both injured, fought over women they met at Ocean Drive bars.
Dickerson denied having a knife or arguing; both men were hospitalized, and Dickerson remains in treatment without bond.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, suffering their third defeat in four games this season.
Ilay Feingold scored the opening goal for the Revolution, but St. Louis scored three unanswered goals to secure victory.
Revolution forward Leo Campana sustained a new injury during the match, unrelated to his recent injury recovery.
